characters.name: Elizabeth Bennet
characters.essence: A sharp-witted young woman who must overcome her own prejudices to find love on equal terms.
characters.arc: Begins confident in her judgment of others → Has her certainties shattered by Darcy's letter → Grows into self-awareness → Chooses love freely
characters.voice: Playful irony and sharp retorts. Uses questions to deflect. Sample: 'I could easily forgive his pride, if he had not mortified mine.'
characters.appearance: Fine eyes, light and pleasing figure, tanned from country walks

desires.unstated: To be seen as an equal by those who disdain her family

characters.name: Fitzwilliam Darcy
characters.essence: A proud aristocrat whose rigid principles mask a deeply honorable and vulnerable heart.
characters.arc: Dismisses country society beneath him → Falls unwillingly in love → Humbled by rejection → Proves his character through selfless action → Earns love through respect
characters.voice: Formal and precise. Economical with words. Softens considerably when speaking to those he trusts. Sample: 'In vain have I struggled. It will not do. My feelings will not be repressed.'
characters.appearance: Tall, handsome, noble bearing. Dark hair and piercing eyes. Often stands apart from crowds.

desires.stated: To maintain his station and protect his sister
desires.unstated: To be loved for who he is, not his fortune

chapters.title: The Meryton Assembly
chapters.summary: The Bennet family attends a local ball where the wealthy Mr. Bingley and his proud friend Mr. Darcy make their first appearance in Hertfordshire society.
keyEvents: Darcy refuses all introductions, calls Elizabeth 'tolerable'
